tasks.title: thenn job: graceful behaviour without systemd + lifecycle hardening
tasks.description:
Context: `thenn job` (recently added) manages scheduled jobs backed by user-level systemd timers (see internal/ job implementation and `thenn job syntax`). It is intended to eventually serve as a wake-timer for long-running agent sessions, so its failure modes matter.

Investigate first:
- How every `thenn job` subcommand (daily/every/once/weekly/weekdays/list/show/run/pause/resume/remove/logs) behaves when `systemctl --user` is unavailable (this container has NO systemd: no systemctl binary, no /run/systemd, no user dbus). Reproduce here.
- Read the job lifecycle code for correctness issues: unit file generation/escaping (commands with quotes/spaces), name collisions, remove of a running job, logs when journald is absent, list parsing when systemctl output is empty/errors.

Deliverables:
1. Every job subcommand fails fast with ONE clear, actionable error when user-level systemd is unavailable (e.g. "thenn job requires user-level systemd (systemctl --user), which is not available here"), instead of raw exec errors or panics. Detection should distinguish "systemctl missing" from "systemd not running / no user bus".
2. Fix any real lifecycle bugs found (escaping, collisions, state edge cases). Keep fixes surgical.
3. Tests: unit tests for the detection seam and unit-file generation; the no-systemd error path must be covered by a test that runs in this container.
4. README/help: document the systemd requirement.

Gates (all must pass): just build, just test, just lint.

tasks.title: thenn job: verification report incl. wake-timer fitness
tasks.description:
Verify the preceding lap's changes by reading the diff and running the gates (just build, just test, just lint), then black-box exercise the real binary (go build to a temp path) in this container: run every `thenn job` subcommand and confirm the graceful no-systemd error appears everywhere with correct exit codes; confirm `thenn job syntax` and help output still render; spot-check unit-file generation via tests or any dry-run seam.

Then write tmp/job-verification-2026-07-10.md (create tmp/) with: commands run, observed output, requirement pass/fail, and a short "wake-timer fitness" assessment: given the code (not runnable here), would `thenn job every 5h -- <command>` reliably fire on a systemd host? Note residual risks (missed timers on suspend, Persistent= setting, RandomizedDelay, what happens if the command is still running at next fire). Do not fix things; report.

tasks.title: thenn job: define reliable wake-timer semantics
tasks.description: Decide and implement the missed-fire and overlap policy needed before presenting `thenn job every` as a reliable agent wake timer. Cover suspend/offline behavior (including whether a realtime persistent timer is needed), make RandomizedDelay policy explicit, define behavior when a prior Type=oneshot command is still running at the next event, document lingering requirements, and add a real-systemd integration test. Follow with a verify lap.

tasks.title: verify thenn wake-timer semantics on real systemd
tasks.description: Review the fixed-delay every policy and calendar catch-up documentation from then-e79d. Run build, unit tests, lint, and the opt-in real-systemd integration test (THENN_SYSTEMD_INTEGRATION=1 go test -tags=integration ./internal/job -run TestRealSystemd) on a host with a reachable systemd --user manager. Confirm OnUnitInactiveSec serialization, one-second accuracy, RandomizedDelaySec=0, WakeSystem=false, removal of persistent timestamp state, suspend/offline claims, and lingering guidance. Record any environment limitations and a verification report.

tasks.title: verify thenn wake-timer semantics on reachable systemd user manager
tasks.description: Run THENN_SYSTEMD_INTEGRATION=1 go test -v -tags=integration ./internal/job -run TestRealSystemd on Linux where systemctl --user show-environment succeeds and XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is set. Record non-skipped evidence of three serialized runs, fixed delay after oneshot completion, and cleanup. Re-run just build, just test, and just lint if the target checkout differs.

tasks.title: verify wake-timer semantics on a reachable systemd user manager
tasks.description: Run THENN_SYSTEMD_INTEGRATION=1 go test -v -tags=integration ./internal/job -run TestRealSystemd on Linux with systemctl installed, systemctl --user show-environment succeeding, and XDG_RUNTIME_DIR set. Record non-skipped evidence of three serialized runs, >=200ms fixed delay after each oneshot completion, and cleanup. This host (2026-07-10) lacks systemctl and XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, so then-473a could only produce a skip.

tasks.title: verify wake-timer semantics on a provisioned reachable systemd user manager
tasks.description: Run on Linux where systemctl is installed, XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is set, and systemctl --user show-environment succeeds. Execute THENN_SYSTEMD_INTEGRATION=1 go test -v -tags=integration ./internal/job -run TestRealSystemd. Capture non-skipped evidence of three serialized oneshot runs, each later start >=200ms after the prior completion, plus cleanup (disable/stop/clean state/unlink/daemon-reload/reset-failed). Current host is unqualified: systemctl absent and XDG_RUNTIME_DIR unset.

tasks.title: verify wake-timer semantics on a provisioned systemd user manager
tasks.description: Run only on Linux with systemctl installed, XDG_RUNTIME_DIR set, and systemctl --user show-environment succeeding. Execute THENN_SYSTEMD_INTEGRATION=1 go test -v -tags=integration ./internal/job -run TestRealSystemd. Record non-skipped output proving three serialized oneshot runs and each later start is >=200ms after previous completion; also verify cleanup executes: disable --now, stop service, clean --what=state, unlink service/timer, daemon-reload, reset-failed. The prior gate skipped because this host lacks systemctl and XDG_RUNTIME_DIR.

tasks.title: verify wake-timer semantics on a qualifying systemd user manager
tasks.description: Blocked verification rerun: current 2026-07-10 Linux host has no systemctl and XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is unset, so the required integration gate was not run. On a Linux host with systemctl installed, XDG_RUNTIME_DIR set, and systemctl --user show-environment succeeding, run: THENN_SYSTEMD_INTEGRATION=1 go test -v -tags=integration ./internal/job -run TestRealSystemd. Capture non-skipped output proving three serialized oneshot runs, every later start is >=200ms after the preceding completion, and cleanup invokes disable --now, stop service, clean --what=state, unlink service/timer, daemon-reload, and reset-failed. Do not treat a skip as passing.

tasks.title: verify wake-timer semantics on a qualifying systemd user manager
tasks.description: Required real-systemd evidence remains unavailable on the 2026-07-10 container: Linux host has no systemctl and XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is unset. On a qualifying Linux host with systemctl installed, XDG_RUNTIME_DIR set, and systemctl --user show-environment succeeding, run THENN_SYSTEMD_INTEGRATION=1 go test -v -tags=integration ./internal/job -run TestRealSystemd. A passing result must be non-skipped and capture three serialized oneshot start/end runs, each later start >=200ms after prior completion, and cleanup activity: disable --now, stop service, clean --what=state, unlink service/timer, daemon-reload, reset-failed. Do not accept a skip as passing.

tasks.title: verify wake-timer semantics on a qualifying systemd user manager
tasks.description: Run only on a Linux host with systemctl installed, non-empty XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, and a successful systemctl --user show-environment. Execute THENN_SYSTEMD_INTEGRATION=1 go test -v -tags=integration ./internal/job -run TestRealSystemd. Passing evidence must be non-skipped and demonstrate three serialized oneshot start/end pairs, each subsequent start at least 200ms after the preceding completion, plus cleanup: disable --now, stop service, clean --what=state, unlink service/timer, daemon-reload, reset-failed. Do not treat a skip as pass.
